reasoning:
The question asks if Fabiano Caruana's rating will be at least 1% higher than his April 13, 2025 rating on four resolution dates. His April rating is 2776. A 1% increase requires an Elo of 2803.76 (meaning 2804 or higher).
Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page updates strictly when official FIDE lists are published, which occurs on the 1st of every month. 
1) By April 20, 2025, the Wikipedia list will still reflect the April 1st ratings. Therefore, his rating will identically be 2776, an increase of 0%. The probability of resolving YES on this date is essentially zero (0.02).
2) By May 13, 2025, the page will reflect the May 1st official FIDE list. To gain 28 points in one month, Caruana would need to perform extraordinarily well (e.g., +5 or +6 over expected in classical games) in April. There are no major classical tournaments slated for him in April that would offer enough rating points to bridge a 28-point gap. This is extremely unlikely (0.04).
3) By July 12, 2025, he will have completed the Norway Chess 2025 tournament among others. While he has previously hit 2803 as recently as January 2025, climbing back to 2804 requires outstanding continuous performance against the absolute top field. The possibility is small but non-negligible (0.12). 
4) By October 10, 2025, over a 6-month horizon, there is an increased chance that typical Elo variance (which caused his drop in Q1 2025) might swing back in his favor, as he is generally a 2790-2800+ level player. However, holding above 2804 exactly on list publication dates remains a difficult feat (0.18).
Probabilities naturally increase with the forecast horizon due to higher variance in possible outcomes.
